titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roberta Yerkes Blanshard Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Diane E. Kaplan and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 30, 2019 at 07:13
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.abstract: The papers consist of subject files, containing correspondence and memorabilia, of Roberta Yerkes Blanshard. The majority of the files relate to the Yale University Press and include a reminiscence of her years with the press. Correspondents in these files include authors with whom she worked, Yale Press staff members, and printers and typographers who worked for the press. There are also files on her friend, Ada Louise Comstock Notestein.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Roberta Yerkes Blanshard, 1998; transferred from Yale University Press, 2013.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Ann Baer in Accession 2013-M-069. Copyright status for the remaining collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roberta Yerkes Blanshard Papers (MS 1566). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Brand Blanshard Papers (MS 1488). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.Robert Mearns Yerkes Papers (MS 569). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Roberta Yerkes Blanshard was born in 1907, the daughter of Robert Mearns and Ada Watterson Yerkes. She attended Bryn Mawr College and joined the Yale University Press staff following graduation in 1929. She remained in the editorial department of the press until 1959. In 1969, she married Brand Blanshard, a Yale professor of philosophy. Blanshard died in Hamden, Connecticut in 2001.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of subject files, containing correspondence and memorabilia, of Roberta Yerkes Blanshard. The majority of the files relate to the Yale University Press and include a reminiscence of her years with the press. Correspondents in these files include authors with whom she worked, Yale Press staff members, and printers and typographers who worked for the press. There are also files on her friend, Ada Louise Comstock Notestein.

did.unittitle: Lecture at Comstock House in Moorhead, Minnesota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The majority of the correspondence consists of letters exchanged by Ann Baer and Roberta Blanshard, 1988-2000. There is also a small amount of Ann Baer's orrespondence with Roland Hoover, Jane Olson, Tina Wheeler, and the Yale University Press. The Press letters discuss her donation of the material and include an explanation of the start of the correspondence.
